page: Amazon Web Services m6g.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features an AWS Graviton2 processor built on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture, running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 4 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, backed by 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache resources include 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across standard compute and memory bandwidth tests, alongside some weaknesses in specialized workloads. In stress-ng testing, the instance achieves average single-core and multi-core CPU scores of 1519.69 and 6053.37, respectively. Large-block memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds of 71.67 GB/sec and 34.85 GB/sec, though small-block cached reads are weaker at 236.28 GB/sec. The instance registers lower-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, as well as in Passmark CPU and encryption tests. Database operations, however, perform at an average level with a Passmark database score of 2614.17.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based Graviton2 architecture delivers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. Because it lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, it is designed for workloads relying on network-attached storage. The instance is suitable for application servers, mid-tier database operations, containerized microservices, and general-purpose web serving where balanced CPU-to-memory ratios are required.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated ARM64 cores powered by an AWS Graviton2 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities in multi-core processing and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside weaker performance in cryptographic and single-core synthetic tests. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads optimized for ARM architecture, making it suitable for general-purpose applications, containerized microservices, and mid-tier database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suitable for balanced microservices and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A balanced ARM64 virtual instance featuring dedicated physical cores and a proportional memory footprint for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton2 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Delivers average performance in database operations and multi-core stress tests
Operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 1 Gbps baseline network speed
Best suited for general-purpose web serving, microservices, and mid-tier databases
